page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture, the instance operates at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates placing in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core and memory-intensive scaling present notable tradeoffs. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and uncached memory bandwidth for large blocks fall into the bottom 25% tier. Static web serving throughput and LLM inference speeds for small and medium models remain in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highmem-4 provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. Due to its strong single-threaded performance but weaker multi-core scaling and database operation benchmarks, it is best suited for single-threaded processing, text processing, light web serving, and memory-heavy applications with low parallelization requirements.

It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The hardware delivers top-tier single-core performance, as demonstrated by high single-threaded compression and Geekbench scores. However, it exhibits weaker multi-core scaling, with stress-ng multi-core metrics and uncached memory bandwidth falling into the bottom 25% tier. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that rely on single-threaded execution. It is suited for text processing, light web serving, and memory-bound tasks with low parallelization demands.
